basicDetails.schemeName: Scholarship Scheme for Girl Children of Workers of Jute Mills/MSME JDP Units

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md:
The National Jute Board Act, 2008, provides to “secure better working conditions and provisions and improvement of amenities and incentives for workers engaged in the jute industry” under Section, 5(2)(xxii) for the welfare of workers in jute-producing units. In line with the same, NJB is going to extend support to the workers’ families to motivate them towards educating their girl children, a scholarship scheme as a Workers’ Welfare measure. 
**Objectives of the Scheme:**  
The objectives of the Scholarship Scheme for Girl Children of Jute Mills / MSMEs Workers are:

- To provide financial incentive to the girl children of the workers of the Jute Mills/MSMEs, upon passing the Secondary and Higher Secondary examinations to encourage them to acquire the minimum required education for making them employable . 
- To encourage workers to work in the jute industry and to remain there for longer period, hence help reduce workers’ attrition rate, which is big problem  of the Industry .
- To help Jute Mills/MSME units to face lesser labour problems .
- The enhance Millers-Workers relationship.  
- To make the workers feel socially protected through such welfare measures for their children .
- To support women empowerment through Girl Children education.
schemeContent.benefits_md:
- Secondary Education (for  Girls only ): ₹ 5,000/- (Five thousand)
- Higher Secondary Education (for  Girls only): ₹ 10,000/- (Ten thousand)
schemeContent.briefDescription: National Jute Board Act, 2008 provides to “secure better working conditions and provisions and improve amenities and incentives for workers engaged in the jute industry.”

children.text: The National Jute Board Act, 2008, provides to “secure better working conditions and provisions and improvement of amenities and incentives for workers engaged in the jute industry” under Section, 5(2)(xxii) for the welfare of workers in jute-producing units. In line with the same, NJB is going to extend support to the workers’ families to motivate them towards educating their girl children, a scholarship scheme as a Workers’ Welfare measure.
Objectives of the Scheme:
The objectives of the Scholarship Scheme for Girl Children of Jute Mills / MSMEs Workers are:

To provide financial incentive to the girl children of the workers of the Jute Mills/MSMEs, upon passing the Secondary and Higher Secondary examinations to encourage them to acquire the minimum required education for making them employable .
To encourage workers to work in the jute industry and to remain there for longer period, hence help reduce workers’ attrition rate, which is big problem  of the Industry .
To help Jute Mills/MSME units to face lesser labour problems .
The enhance Millers-Workers relationship.
To make the workers feel socially protected through such welfare measures for their children .
To support women empowerment through Girl Children education.
Secondary Education (for  Girls only ): ₹ 5,000/- (Five thousand)
Higher Secondary Education (for  Girls only): ₹ 10,000/- (Ten thousand)
Step 1:

The parents/guardians should visit (during office hours) the respective Jute Mills/JDP Units Officer and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
Step 2:
In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Step 3:
Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the respective Jute Mills/JDP Units.

applicationProcess.process_md: **Step 1:** The parents/guardians should visit (during office hours) the respective Jute Mills/JDP Units Officer and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications. **Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required). **Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the respective Jute Mills/JDP Units.

schemeDefinitions.name: Jute Mills

children.text: Any registered Jute Mill manufacturing jute products will be considered under the scheme.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md: Any registered Jute Mill manufacturing jute products will be considered under the scheme.

schemeDefinitions.name: MSME- JDP Unit

children.text: Any unit holding  Registration cum Membership Certificate (RCMC) of JPDEPC as “Manufacturer-Exporter’ or having a valid factory license and has paid a license fee for the current year or a unit having Part – 2 registration under MSME and employing more than 10 workers in production unit will be considered under the scheme.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md: Any unit holding  Registration cum Membership Certificate (RCMC) of JPDEPC as “Manufacturer-Exporter’ or having a valid factory license and has paid a license fee for the current year or a unit having Part – 2 registration under MSME and employing more than 10 workers in production unit will be considered under the scheme.

schemeDefinitions.name: Worker of a Mill

children.text: Any workman directly engaged in manufacturing jute goods or involved in the shop floor activities of jute goods producing units and in the payroll, as declared by the mill.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md: Any workman directly engaged in manufacturing jute goods or involved in the shop floor activities of jute goods producing units and in the payroll, as declared by the mill.

schemeDefinitions.name: Worker of a JDP Unit

children.text: Any worker who is directly engaged in the manufacture of  JDPs and is on the payroll of the JDP unit and enrolled under ESI of the beneficiary unit.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md: Any worker who is directly engaged in the manufacture of  JDPs and is on the payroll of the JDP unit and enrolled under ESI of the beneficiary unit.

schemeDefinitions.name: Children

children.text: Children means only the girl children of a worker who passed the Secondary/Higher Secondary Examination under Board/Council/College/ University through a recognized school/college/University.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md: Children means only the girl children of a worker who passed the Secondary/Higher Secondary Examination under Board/Council/College/ University through a recognized school/college/University.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md:
- Scholarship to the girl children on Passing out the Examination in 2021(2021-22), 2022 (2022-23), 2023 (2023-24), 2024 (2024-25), 2025 (2025-26) - Total 5 Years (2021-26) 15th Finance Commission period.
- The scheme will be applicable to the girl children of the workers of Jute Mills and MSME - JDP units engaged in the production of jute products.

children.text: Scholarship to the girl children on Passing out the Examination in 2021(2021-22), 2022 (2022-23), 2023 (2023-24), 2024 (2024-25), 2025 (2025-26) - Total 5 Years (2021-26) 15th Finance Commission period.
The scheme will be applicable to the girl children of the workers of Jute Mills and MSME - JDP units engaged in the production of jute products.
